---
title: "Configuration"
description: "Configure route scanning and generated output paths for svelte-auto-href."
---

Most SvelteKit applications only need `ts href()` with no options:

```ts name="vite.config.ts" icon="vite"
import { sveltekit } from "@sveltejs/kit/vite";
import { defineConfig } from "vite";
import { href } from "svelte-auto-href";

export default defineConfig({
	plugins: [href(), sveltekit()],
});
```

## Generated files

The default generation pass writes:

| File                                             | Purpose                                           |
| ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------- |
| `.svelte-kit/svelte-auto-href/manifest.json`     | Route records for editor and integration tooling. |
| `.svelte-kit/svelte-auto-href/html-data.json`    | Native markup attribute completions.              |
| `.svelte-kit/types/svelte-auto-href/$types.d.ts` | SvelteKit and package type augmentations.         |

Everything stays beneath SvelteKit's generated directory, so these files should not be committed.

## Plugin options

Override paths when the application uses a custom source layout or another tool owns part of `icon="folder" .svelte-kit`:

```ts name="vite.config.ts" icon="vite"
import { sveltekit } from "@sveltejs/kit/vite";
import { defineConfig } from "vite";
import { href } from "svelte-auto-href";

export default defineConfig({
	plugins: [
		href({
			routes_dir: "src/routes",
			output_dir: ".svelte-kit/svelte-auto-href",
			types_path: ".svelte-kit/types/svelte-auto-href/$types.d.ts",
		}),
		sveltekit(),
	],
});
```

| Option       | Default                                          | Meaning                                          |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `routes_dir` | `src/routes`                                     | Route directory relative to the Vite root.       |
| `output_dir` | `.svelte-kit/svelte-auto-href`                   | Directory for the manifest and HTML custom data. |
| `types_path` | `.svelte-kit/types/svelte-auto-href/$types.d.ts` | Generated declaration file.                      |

Relative paths resolve from Vite's project root. Absolute paths are accepted by the generator API, though keeping generated files inside the application makes editor and cleanup behavior easier to understand.

## TypeScript inclusion

The default declaration path is already included by SvelteKit's generated TypeScript config. Keep the usual project config:

```json name="tsconfig.json" icon="typescript"
{
	"extends": "./.svelte-kit/tsconfig.json"
}
```

If you move `types_path`, include the new declaration location yourself. The plugin can generate a correct file that TypeScript never reads, so this is the first place to check when completions disappear after customization.

## Applications with a base path

The scanner describes the route tree with root pathnames such as `/account/settings`. It does not read `ts kit.paths.base`, because that deployment prefix belongs to SvelteKit rather than the route ID.

Resolve a suggested route through SvelteKit before rendering or navigating to it:

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
	import { resolve } from "$app/paths";
	import { strict_href } from "svelte-auto-href";

	const account_href = resolve(strict_href("/account/settings"));
</script>

<a href={account_href}>Account settings</a>
```

`ts resolve()` prefixes the configured base and also handles relative server-rendered paths.

## Plugin composition

With `svelte-plugin-composer`, the recommended order places `ts href()` before `ts kit()`. With ordinary Vite plugins, place it before `ts sveltekit()`. The generator does not need a Svelte preprocessor and does not change application runtime code.

For programmatic generation outside Vite, use `ts generate_auto_href()` from `ts "svelte-auto-href/generator"`; the [API reference](/docs/auto-href/api-reference) covers the returned paths and lower-level integration exports.
